what_it_does: Declares a single RELATION_TYPE dict named 'blocked_by' defining a directed board edge meaning 'X is blocked by Y' — a conceptual dependency relation between addressed perspectives/claims on the board.
observations: RELATION_TYPE fields: id='blocked_by', directed=True, label='blocked-by', desc.
desc says X is blocked by Y (a claim/work item gated on an unmet dependency).
Framed as a CONCEPTUAL edge between addressed perspectives/claims (the harvest's RELATIONS), kept first-class so the linking layer parses it rather than collapsing it to 'references'.
Stated to be surfaced by-use during the wind-down harvest (recollection).
standouts: The deliberate refusal to collapse this into a generic 'references' edge — it is kept first-class.
conventions: Single-dict module shape (RELATION_TYPE with id/directed/label/desc), the board-edge registry-row convention.
id uses snake_case while label uses hyphenated form.
notes: A first-class directed 'blocked-by' dependency edge type for the board, harvested from session wind-down.
questions: What other board_edges relation types exist alongside this, and do they share the same harvest/recollection origin?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: declares exactly the blocked-by relation type its desc describes.
apparent_themes: First-class typed relations (resisting collapse to generic references)
Conceptual/dependency modeling of claims and work items
intention_signals: Trying to preserve a specific dependency/blocking relationship between claims as a distinct, parseable edge rather than flattening it, so blocking structure is legible on the board.
contribution: Contributes the directed 'blocked-by' dependency edge type definition for the board's relation registry, including the rationale for keeping it distinct from 'references'.
summary_for_embedding: A Python registry-row defining a directed 'blocked-by' relation type between board claims, kept first-class rather than collapsed into a generic references edge.
intention_for_embedding: To represent unmet dependencies between claims/work items as a distinct, parseable board edge surfaced during the wind-down harvest.
